text:
Eagle-eyedOlympicsfans spotted a moment of true sportsmanship in the immediate aftermath ofAlex Yee’s stunning gold in the men’s triathlonthis morning.

New Zealand’s Hayden Wilde appeared to have one hand on the top prize as he opened up a sizeable lead over the rest of the field after the swimming and cycling events, with Team GB’s Yee 14 seconds adrift with one lap of the 10km run remaining.

French fans began roaring on their home favourite, Leo Bergere, who appeared to be closing the gap on Yee, but the British athlete held firm and began eating up ground on Wilde, with the finishing line coming into view.

And when Yee squeezed past Wilde, the Kiwi athlete simply had nothing left to give – his legs seemingly gone after a gruelling and compelling battle over the three disciplines.

Yee – who won silver in Tokyo three years ago – collapsed in a heap after storming home to sealTeam GB’s fifth gold medal of the Games and the 26-year-old athlete was soon joined by Wilde on the track.

After Wilde was able to catch his breath, he showed his class by throwing an arm around Yee, congratulating his Lewisham-born rival on an incredible victory.

Bergere – who cliched bronze – soon followed suit and pictures of the trio immediately began doing the rounds on social media, with some spectators claiming the moment summed up the spirit of the Games.

Respect ð¤Hayden Wilde sits down to congratulate Alex Yee after their epic battle in the men’s triathlon.This is what the#Olympicsis all about. â¤ï¸ï¸#BBCOlympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/yajCiupR5f

‘This is what true sportsmanship is all about. Hayden Wilde must be devastated but his first thought is to wrap an arm around Alex Yee. Incredible run fro Yee,’ wrote @KDCunliffe on X, formerly known as Twitter.

‘One of the moments of Olympic Games so far. Alex Yee wins an astonishing triathlon – after looking completely beaten, 14 secs down. And then is congratulated with genuine warmth by Hayden Wilde, the NZ guy he beat,’ @hwallop tweeted.

‘Image of Olympics for me. True sportsmanship from Wilde, after seeing Alex Yee take the triathlon gold from him in the last 400m,’ @jonnypitchfork said.

Meanwhile, @BeckyFlashHart wrote: ‘Wilde. Long time leader of the men’s triathlon. Passed in the final straight. And immediately when crossing the line sat next to Yee and put his arm round him. He must be gutted and yet his first act was to congratulate his opponent. Class. Pure class.’

Speaking shortly after the race, Wilde passed on his congratulations to Yee and said he was ‘stoked’ for the Brit.

‘It was just a great race overall. He slowed up at 2km in, and I knew that was my time to go,’ Wilde said.

‘I got the gap and pushed and pushed and tried and tried. But I missed out at the end.

‘I gave it everything, I can’t leave this race with a “what if?”.

‘I’m just stoked for Alex as well. We’ve both choked in big grand finals. It’s really nice to pull one off for him and I.’

title: Olympics fans praise Hayden Wilde's classy reaction to epic Alex Yee triathlon win | Metro News
description: Fans spotted a moment of true sportsmanship in the aftermath of Alex Yee's stunning Olympic gold in the men's triathlon this morning.
